summary:
Cross-sectional studies assess various participants' attributes contemporaneously within defined categories like age groupings, providing snapshots devoid of long-term tracking found in longitudinal approaches. These studies aim for immediate comparisons across diverse yet categorized individual samples at a precise moment.
The answer is consistent with Option B highlighting simultaneous assessments of varied individuals fitting into specified demographic segments during a brief period. Agent2 has agreed upon this interpretation after reviewing pertinent definitions and contrasts between types of observational epidemiological methods. To summarize:

To directly address your request while adhering strictly to two concise sentences focusing only on critical aspects related to answering whether 'cross-sectional studies carry out':

Cross-sectional studies evaluate multiple people sharing certain traits such as ages all measured together at once, offering insights through concurrent observations instead of sequential monitoring seen elsewhere. They provide timely snapshots comparing differing subject pools under unified conditions briefly captured. [Option B] captures accurately how these surveys operate specifically targeting contemporary analyses involving assorted demographics instantly observed collectively.
